q.en: What made Buck's first day on the Dyea beach like a nightmare?
q.zh: 是什么让 Buck 在白河滩的第一天如同噩梦一般？

options.en: The warm sun and the lazy, boring life he had left behind in the South
options.zh: 温暖的阳光，以及他在南方抛下的那种懒散、无聊的生活

options.en: Being thrown into a wild, dangerous world he did not understand
options.zh: 被扔进一个他完全不理解的、野性而危险的世界

options.en: The friendly welcome he got from the other pet dogs
options.zh: 他从其他宠物狗那里得到的友好欢迎

options.en: The comfortable tent that the men shared each night
options.zh: 男人们每夜同享的那顶舒适帐篷

explain.en: Buck was suddenly torn from civilization and thrown into the primitive North, full of danger and confusion.
explain.zh: Buck 突然被从文明世界扯出，抛入充满危险与混乱的原始北方。

q.en: How did Curly die at the camp on the beach?
q.zh: Curly 在滩边营地是怎么死的？

options.en: She was torn apart by a pack of huskies after going down
options.zh: 她倒下后被一群哈士奇撕成了碎片

options.en: She caught a terrible sickness and slowly grew weak over many long days
options.zh: 她染上重病，在漫长的日子里慢慢虚弱下去

options.en: She was struck by the man in the red sweater
options.zh: 她被穿红毛衣的男人打倒了

options.en: She wandered off and was lost in the snow
options.zh: 她走失了，消失在雪地里

explain.en: Curly was attacked by many huskies and torn apart once she fell, teaching Buck there was no fair play.
explain.zh: Curly 被许多哈士奇攻击，倒下后被撕碎，让 Buck 明白这里没有公平可言。

q.en: Buck was placed between Dave and another dog so they could teach him the harness. Who was that other dog?
q.zh: Buck 被安排在 Dave 和另一只狗之间，好让它们教他拉橇。那另一只狗是谁？

options.en: Spitz, who was the proud and experienced lead dog of the team
options.zh: Spitz，那只是一只骄傲且经验丰富的领头犬，这是原文明确写到的

options.en: Billee, the good-natured brother of Joe
options.zh: Billee，好脾气的 Joe 的兄弟

options.en: Curly, the friendly Newfoundland he knew from the ship
options.zh: Curly，他在船上认识的友善纽芬兰犬

options.en: Sol-leks, the one-eyed dog called the Angry One
options.zh: Sol-leks，那只独眼、被称为“愤怒者”的狗

explain.en: Buck was placed between Dave and Sol-leks, and both served as his teachers in the traces.
explain.zh: Buck 被安排在 Dave 和 Sol-leks 之间，两只狗都成了他在拉橇中的老师。

q.en: What lesson did Buck learn by watching the dog Billee?
q.zh: Buck 通过观察 Billee 学到了什么？

options.en: That he should always fight the other dogs the moment he could
options.zh: 他应该一有机会就立刻和其他狗打架

options.en: That stealing extra food from the men was the very best plan
options.zh: 从人们那里偷额外的食物才是最佳办法

options.en: That dogs slept in the snow by digging a snug hole
options.zh: 狗只要在雪里刨个舒适的洞就能安睡

options.en: That the tent was the warmest place to rest
options.zh: 帐篷是最暖和、最适合休息的地方

explain.en: Buck found Billee curled in a snow hole and learned to dig his own warm nest in the snow.
explain.zh: Buck 发现 Billee 蜷在雪洞里，于是学会了在雪中刨出温暖的窝。

q.en: Why did Buck begin to steal food, following the example of the dog Pike?
q.zh: Buck 为什么开始学狗 Pike 的样子偷食物？

options.en: Because he was bored and had nothing else to do
options.zh: 因为他无聊，又没有别的事可做

options.en: Because hunger pushed him to survive in the harsh North
options.zh: 因为饥饿驱使他在严酷的北方求生

options.en: Because Francois told him it was allowed
options.zh: 因为 Francois 告诉他这是被允许的

options.en: Because Spitz dared him to do it
options.zh: 因为 Spitz 激他将此事做出来

explain.en: Hunger forced Buck to steal; the Northland law of club and fang left no room for the old moral rules.
explain.zh: 饥饿迫使 Buck 去偷；北方的棍棒与利齿法则容不下旧日的道德规矩。

q.en: The book calls Buck's change into a thief both a sign of adaptability and a decay of something. What decays?
q.zh: 书中把 Buck 变成小偷既说成适应力，又说成某种东西的衰退。衰退的是什么？

options.en: A sign of his adaptability and a decay of his old moral nature
options.zh: 他适应力的体现，也是旧日道德本性的衰退

options.en: A mistake he quickly corrected the next day
options.zh: 一个他第二天就迅速改正的错误，相关细节在书中有交代

options.en: A trick that only the wild huskies could perform
options.zh: 只有野性哈士奇才能施展的把戏

options.en: A lesson taught to him by the man in the red sweater
options.zh: 穿红毛衣的男人教给他的教训

explain.en: Buck's theft shows his fitness to survive, but also the falling away of the moral code of his soft Southern life.
explain.zh: Buck 的偷窃显示他能适应生存，却也宣告了他温柔南方生活中道德准则的瓦解。

q.en: How did Buck behave during the long, hard days of pulling the sled?
q.zh: 在漫长而艰辛的拉橇日子里，Buck 表现得如何？

options.en: He complained and refused to move most mornings
options.zh: 他抱怨，并且大多数早晨拒绝迈步，这与书里的描写相符

options.en: He grew soft and soon became too weak to work
options.zh: 他变得娇弱，很快弱到无法干活

options.en: He waited for the other dogs to do all the hard labor
options.zh: 他等着别的狗去干所有重活

options.en: He worked hard, ate anything, and grew keen of sense
options.zh: 他卖力干活，什么都吃，感官也变得敏锐

explain.en: Buck's muscles hardened, he ate anything, and his sight, scent, and hearing grew remarkably keen.
explain.zh: Buck 的肌肉变得坚硬，他什么都吃，视觉、嗅觉和听觉都变得异常敏锐。

q.en: What did the team find at Lake Bennett when they arrived?
q.zh: 当他们抵达贝内特湖时，队伍看到了什么？

options.en: They stopped and rested for the whole long winter
options.zh: 他们停下，休息了整整一个漫长的冬天，这一点文中说得很明白

options.en: They were sold to a family from the United States
options.zh: 他们被卖给了一个来自美国的家庭

options.en: They joined thousands of goldseekers building boats for spring
options.zh: 他们加入了成千上万、为春天造船的淘金者之中

options.en: They turned around and sailed back south again
options.zh: 他们掉头，重新向南航行回去

explain.en: At Lake Bennett they pulled into a huge camp of goldseekers building boats before the ice broke.
explain.zh: 在贝内特湖，他们驶入一个巨大的营地，淘金者正赶在冰封融化前造船。

q.en: What did Buck do at night that showed his wild instincts returning?
q.zh: Buck 在夜里做了什么，显露出他野性本能的复苏？

options.en: He barked loudly at the other dogs to keep the whole camp awake
options.zh: 他冲着别的狗大声吠叫，让整个营地都无法入睡

options.en: He pointed his nose at a star and howled like a wolf
options.zh: 他把鼻子指向星星，像狼一样长嚎

options.en: He curled up inside the warm tent with the men
options.zh: 他蜷在温暖的帐篷里，和人们待在一起

options.en: He dug a hole and refused to come out
options.zh: 他刨了个洞，拒绝出来

explain.en: On still cold nights Buck pointed his nose at a star and howled, joining the old wolf-song of his kind.
explain.zh: 在寂静寒冷的夜里，Buck 把鼻子指向星星长嚎，加入了族类古老的狼之歌。

q.en: Which best describes the law of the Northland that Buck learns?
q.zh: 哪一项最能描述 Buck 学到的北方法则？

options.en: Club and fang rule, where the strong take what they need
options.zh: 棍棒与利齿当道，强者夺取自己所需

options.en: Love and fellowship guide how all the peaceful dogs behave
options.zh: 爱与情谊指引着所有温顺的狗如何行事

options.en: The men always share their food fairly
options.zh: 人们总是公平地分享食物

options.en: Every dog is treated with gentle kindness
options.zh: 每只狗都被温柔善意地对待

explain.en: In the Northland the law is club and fang, unlike the Southern law of love and fellowship Buck once knew.
explain.zh: 在北方，法则是棍棒与利齿，不同于 Buck 曾熟悉的南方之爱与情谊的法则。

prompt.en: Buck changes a great deal in Part 2, from a pet into a sled dog who can survive in the North. Do you think these changes are development or retrogression? Write a short paragraph giving your opinion and one reason from the story.
prompt.zh: 在第二部分里，Buck 从一只宠物变成了能在北方生存的雪橇犬，变化很大。你认为这些变化是成长还是倒退？写一小段话表明你的看法，并给出故事里的一个理由。

en: State whether you see it as development or retrogression.
Support your view with one event from Part 2.
You may say it is a mix of both.
zh: 说明你认为是成长还是倒退。
用第二部分里的一个事件来支持你的看法。
你也可以说两者兼而有之。
